title:
Junior Software Engineering Trainer
Junior Software Engineering Trainer with verification
description:
About the job
Become a Software Engineering Trainer and Coach at FDM Australia
Strong in programming but drawn to coaching? This is your chance to turn your technical expertise into real impact.

For 10 years, FDM has built the talent behind Australia’s tech and innovation sectors, supporting leading organisations across banking, finance, insurance, retail and more. Now we’re looking for a Software Engineering Trainer to help shape the next generation.

You’ll prepare early-career graduates for Software Engineering roles, mentoring them into confident, client-ready Technical Consultants. Beyond core engineering skills, we’re investing heavily in AI fluency. You’ll build foundations in prompt engineering, strengthen your own capability, and help consultants apply AI in practical, project-based environments.


How it works 
Before stepping into the role, you’ll complete 10+ weeks of focused upskilling in our Skills Lab. You’ll then join a collaborative team of Coaches across APAC, delivering hands-on, real-world training.

What you’ll do 
Design and deliver structured, hands-on training in the FDM Skills Lab that reflects real client environments
Design projects aligned to industry demand and learning outcomes
Mentor consultants within pod teams, guiding problem-solving, growth and project readiness
Provide clear, tailored feedback to build confidence and technical capability
Assess performance through practical evaluations and interviews
Support recruitment by assessing candidate aptitude and conducting mock interviews
Prepare consultants for client assignments, including resume readiness and professional presentation
Partner with Sales as a technical SME in client conversations
If you want to grow your own expertise while shaping Australia’s future software engineers, this is the role to do it.


Why join us?
Play a direct role in launching the next generation of software engineers
Be part of a dedicated team of Coaches across the APAC region
Receive structured upskilling to excel in your coaching role
Gain hands-on experience mentoring and developing early-career talent
Access upskilling opportunities (computer science background required)
Access career growth opportunities within FDM while shaping future tech leaders


What we look for
Degree or higher in a tech-related field
Experience with one or more of the following: Java, SQL, Unix, Python
Commercial experience is a plus, but not essential
Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly
Patient, supportive, and confident mentoring early-career talent
Commitment to at least 2 years with FDM, helping you grow while shaping future tech talent
Ability to work in a collaborative, fast-paced learning environment


If you’re passionate about developing people and want to help shape the future of tech talent in Australia, we’d love to hear from you.



About FDM
FDM powers the people behind tech and innovation. We spot trends, find top talent, and help businesses stay ahead.
With 35+ years of experience, we coach, mentor, and launch fresh thinkers from diverse backgrounds into world-class careers. Partnering with top global companies, we deliver the right talent at the right time, while guiding our people toward exponential growth.
🌍 Global impact – 18 centres across APAC, North America, the UK & Europe
🚀 25,000+ careers launched – and counting
🤝 300+ trusted client partners


Committed to Diversity, Equity & Inclusion
Tech careers should be for everyone. With 75+ nationalities represented, FDM thrives on diversity, fuels innovation through unique perspectives, and celebrates success together. As an Equal Opportunity Employer and FTSE4Good-listed company, we ensure every qualified applicant gets a fair shot, no barriers, just opportunities.
